signals.title: Trump executive order directs NASA to plan quantum space applications
signals.summary:
The White House has issued an executive order aiming to unify and accelerate U.S. development of quantum technologies, including space systems that could enable next-generation navigation, sensing and secure communications. 
 The post  Trump executive order directs NASA to plan quantum space applications  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.summary: Rheinmetall and other European defense stocks fell for a second day, after Germany scrapped the F126 naval program, raising procurement concerns.

signals.title: Gen. Christopher Donahue to unexpectedly relinquish command of Army Europe and Africa
signals.summary: U.S. Army Gen. Christopher Donahue will unexpectedly relinquish command next week after just 18 months in the role, the Pentagon confirmed Wednesday.

signals.title: Power plants under DOE emergency orders are producing way less energy than before
signals.summary: The Department of Energy ordered six power plants to delay their retirements last year. Two of them produced zero electricity in the first quarter in 2026, and another one is now offline for repairs.

signals.title: 3 home energy providers offer 16.8 GW of distributed capacity to utilities, hyperscalers
signals.summary: An executive for Sunrun said active “distributed power plant” programs like one in Puerto Rico underscore the resource’s potential.

signals.title: Platte River, EnergyHub partner to deploy 39-MW Colorado VPP
signals.summary: Platte River Power Authority plans to achieve 19 MW from customer-owned distributed resources by 2030, with the first virtual power plant programs launching this summer.

signals.title: Grid operators making ‘significant progress’ on generator interconnection reform: AEU
signals.summary: However, there isn’t yet evidence that interconnection requests are being processed more quickly, according to Advanced Energy United.

signals.title: Iran warns ships it's 'unacceptable and dangerous' to transit the Strait of Hormuz without their approval
signals.summary: The stern warning underscores Tehran's resolve to retain control over the Strait of Hormuz and to resist transits that bypass its authorization.

signals.title: Oil prices erase wartime gains as supply concerns ease with Hormuz tanker traffic resuming
signals.summary: Oil prices fell as investors bet global crude supplies will improve, as tankers stranded in the Persian Gulf for months have begun leaving the Strait of Hormuz.

signals.title: Anthropic's latest hiring spree reveals where it's building AI data centers next
signals.summary: The AI lab is hiring for AI data center roles in Australia and Japan as it rushes to expand compute capacity overseas.

signals.title: One of India's largest gold exporters paid its managing director just $180 a month, investigators say in accounting probe
signals.summary: Indian authorities have found accounting discrepancies in books of Indian gold company Rajesh Exports, and owner of Swiss refiner Valcambi.

signals.title: US sees record Q1 2026 energy storage installations amid rosy outlook
signals.summary: Relative certainty around tax policy and demand from large load customers are among factors driving the country’s energy storage boom, according to two reports out this month.

signals.summary: The contracts offer a way for school districts to lock in costs as electricity prices rise, says Louis Maltezos, co-president of the energy infrastructure company.

signals.title: Frontier Climate adds Anthropic to buyers group, makes new financing pledge
signals.summary: The company behind AI tool Claude joins the carbon removal purchasing group, which also announced $915 million in new financing commitments.

signals.title: The AI race will be won or lost on power infrastructure
signals.summary: If the harder AI constraint turns out to be infrastructure performance rather than generation, the power sector may be looking for the bottleneck in the wrong place, writes TerraFlow Energy CMO Amanda Simonian.

signals.title: TVA considers up to 26 GW of gas-fired generation
signals.summary: Load growth in the Tennessee Valley Authority region is already outpacing the reference case forecast that was in its draft integrated resource plan, the federal utility said.

signals.summary: The countries said Chinese patrols off the coast of Taiwan threaten regional stability and freedom of navigation.

signals.title: China studied US stealth aircraft — and learned the wrong lessons
signals.summary: An Air Force researcher said China's assessments of U.S. stealth capabilities "diverge from technical reality and operational practice."

signals.title: Airstrike killed senior ISIS commander in Syria, CENTCOM says
signals.summary: A senior Islamic State leader was killed by an airstrike last week, Central Command announced on Wednesday.

signals.title: Germany scraps F126 frigate program, pivots to MEKO warships amid cost and contractor chaos
signals.summary: Germany Navy leaders have already blessed the fallback ships as capable of meeting NATO capability requirements, but an alliance deadline looms.

signals.title: Iran's neighbor, U.S. ally: What Pakistan gains from being a peacemaker
signals.summary: Pakistan's efforts to bring an end to the conflict in the Middle East are driven by the need to avoid a spillover across its borders.

signals.title: SK Hynix surges 12% after Micron earnings; blockbuster Nasdaq listing
signals.summary: Shares of South Korean chip giant SK Hynix surged 12% on Thursday after the company said it is seeking to raise as much as $29.4 billion.

signals.title: Inside India newsletter: Meet the humans teaching robots to perform routine tasks, as India finds a way to enter the AI race
signals.summary: Several companies have cropped up in India providing video training data made by humans that is being used to teach robots in the U.S. and China.

signals.title: South Korea’s IPO bust clouds equity markets as Chaebol structure restrains listings
signals.summary: South Korea's IPO activity has lagged that of its regional peers as the structure of the market and governance reforms collide.

signals.title: Investors still seek a human touch even with AI tools at hand: HSBC
signals.summary: Investors are still turning to the judgment of a professional adviser in their last investment decision, despite using AI tools, a HSBC survey found.

signals.title: Identity theft victims face 'unconscionable' IRS delays, report says
signals.summary: Victims currently face wait times of almost two years to resolve their cases, according to the National Taxpayer Advocate.

signals.title: Federal Reserve says U.S. banks can withstand $708 billion in losses amid overhaul of capital rules
signals.summary: The Fed's annual exercise comes at a pivotal moment for bank regulation because, unlike previous years, the results will not affect capital requirements.

signals.title: Qualcomm stock pops 15% after chipmaker almost doubles projection for 2029 non-handset revenue
signals.summary: Qualcomm's primary business has been smartphones, which accounted for two-thirds of the company's product revenue in it's most recent quarter.

signals.title: This industrial play has been a quiet winner in 2026. Why the stock has more room to go
signals.summary: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

signals.summary: The letter, which was obtained by CNBC, claims Alibaba carried out "the largest known distillation attack on Anthropic to date."

signals.title: Cerebras CEO says margin forecast was 'misunderstood' as stock plummets after earnings
signals.summary: AI chipmaker Cerebras has a staggered lock-up expiration that includes some shares becoming available for trading this week.

signals.summary: Toyota has notably leaned into hybrid vehicles while GM and others bet on all-electric vehicles, which saw lower-than-expected adoption from consumers.

signals.summary: The Army has successfully tested software designed to help vehicle-mounted weapons shoot down drones while both the target and the vehicle are moving.

signals.title: Frustrating Israel, fiber-optic killer drone technology has arrived in southern Lebanon
signals.summary: Analysts believe that any military engaged in conflict will, in one form or another, encounter technologies first seen in Ukraine.

signals.title: India’s three new naval ships boost maritime firepower
signals.summary: The country has made strides in constructing complex naval platforms but remains dependent on foreign suppliers for key technologies.

signals.summary: Autonomous boats helped escort a U.S. Army vessel during a military exercise, in a demonstration of how uncrewed assets are changing maritime operations.

signals.title: Kyiv’s drone leverage moved the US. Moscow could be next, a top Ukrainian official says.
signals.summary: After years of needing Western weapons, Kyiv is approaching negotiations with battlefield tech the U.S. now wants — and a possible ceasefire looming.

signals.summary: Battle-damaged U.S. warships could not be quickly repaired and returned to combat during a war with China, according to a new study by RAND.

signals.title: Can China’s latest air-to-air missile take on its US equivalent? Definitely maybe, experts say.
signals.summary: The PL-16’s specs would let it chase large, slower-moving aircraft, one analyst said, though its effectiveness against smaller, faster planes is unclear.
